title: stipa diastrophica
intro: Stipa diastrophica, also known as the prairie junegrass, is a perennial grass species native to the northern Great Plains of North America. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been attributed to its essential oils and flavonoids. The plant has been valued for its health benefits, such as reducing fever and alleviating respiratory issues, and has been used in herbal preparations, including teas and tinctures. However, more research is needed to fully understand the efficacy and potential interactions of Stipa diastrophica with other medications.
